title: rosa gallica subvar. pumila
intro: Rosa gallica subvar. pumila, also known as Dwarf French rose or Rosa gallica 'Petit', is a compact, deciduous shrub that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rosa gallica subvar. pumila include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to treat various ailments, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
